summary: India operates several large-scale digital social protection programs linked through the Aadhaar identity system. PM-KISAN provides direct benefit transfers to over 110 million farmers. Jan Dhan Yojana has enabled 500 million+ bank accounts for financial inclusion. The Public Distribution System (PDS) uses Aadhaar-based biometric authentication for subsidized food grain distribution. However, significant gaps remain in rural connectivity and digital literacy, leading to exclusion errors.

sources.title: Jan Dhan Yojana - Department of Financial Services

notes: The JAM trinity (Jan Dhan-Aadhaar-Mobile) forms the backbone of India's digital social protection delivery. Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) platform has disbursed over INR 30 lakh crore across 300+ schemes. Rural connectivity and biometric authentication failures remain challenges in remote areas.
